description: “We need to make Russia wake up each morning, thinking to themselves: 'Not today,'" the alliance's top officer for force transformation said.

title: Joy and heartbreak as World Cup qualifying marathon ends
description: The 48-team field for FIFA ’s biggest ever World Cup is complete after a qualifying process that spanned more than two and a half years.  Iraq edged Bolivia 2-1 in an intercontinental playoff at Monterrey in northern Mexico on Tuesday to secure the 48th spot, hours after Bosnia and Herzegovina upset four-time champion Italy on penalties in European playoffs. The result meant Italy was knocked out in qualifying for a third successive World Cup.

title: French truck drivers protest over rising fuel prices

title: Jailed Palestinians fear death by hanging without due process under new Israeli law
description: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank voiced fears on Tuesday that their jailed relatives could be hanged without due process after Israel adopted a new law ​making the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks. According to Mr. OWEIS, the humanitarian situation in the region is deteriorating at an alarming pace, particularly for children. What we are witnessing is not only an escalation in violence, but the normalisation of suffering: a condition in which displacement, trauma, and the loss of basic services are becoming embedded in everyday life. title: Death penalty bill an 'extension of the genocidal measures of the State of Israel': Palestinian FM
description: Speaking to FRANCE 24, Palestinian Foreign Minister Varsen Aghabekian condemned Israel’s new death penalty bill as “an extension of the genocidal measures of the State of Israel”. She accused settlers and the Israeli army of being “one and the same”, and insisted that the two-state solution remains the “only solution”.

title: Israel says will reduce French defence imports 'to zero'
description: Israel will reduce its military imports from France "to zero" in retaliation for French policies it deems hostile towards its interests, a defence ministry spokeswoman told AFP on Tuesday. According to the French parliament, Paris does not export weapons to Israel, but components to be used in defence systems or to be re-exported to third countries. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: Bedouins in Israel’s Negev desert face bomb shelter shortage
description: As Iranian and Hezbollah air strikes on Israel continue, tens of thousands of Bedouins living in the country’s southern Negev desert have no access to shelters. While some NGOs are attempting to install mobile shelters, they are primarily calling on the government to take action, denouncing "discriminatory planning policies".

title: French Film Show: ‘Mrs’ lays bare bourgeois family tensions
description: The weight of tradition bears heavily on Marianne, the protagonist of the new film "Mrs", played by Mélanie Thierry as a seemingly perfect wife and mother. Film critic Manon Kerjean explains how director David Roux’s minimalist approach draws on the legacy of bourgeois drama master Claude Chabrol – and why Thierry’s performance carries the film.

title: Eurovision Song Contest expanding with Asian edition
description: The music spectacle Eurovision is holding its first Asian edition in Bangkok later this year. The Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026 has confirmed artists from at least 10 countries across Asia competing: Thailand, South Korea, the Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Cambodia, Laos, Bangladesh, Nepal and Bhutan. More are expected to join before the finale in November.

title: Transatlantic rift widens as Trump lashes out at NATO

description: The group is suspected of involvement in a string of attacks on Jewish targets in Belgium, Britain and the Netherlands.

title: Иранский металлургический завод серьезно пострадал в результате атак
description: Удары по металлургическому заводу в Исфахане, принадлежащему иранской компании Mobarakeh Steel, привели к существенным разрушениям на предприятии, сообщает в среду Bloomberg."По имеющимся на данный момент оценкам, атаки были массированными, нанесли значительный ущерб, привели к серьезному разрушению производственных объектов", - приводит агентство выдержку из заявления компании.Bloomberg напоминает, что завод уже подвергался ударам в конце прошло

title: В Исфахане значительные повреждения получил металлургический завод
description: В Исфахане в результате бомбежек значительные повреждения получил металлургический завод иранской Mobarakeh Steel, сообщает Bloomberg со ссылкой на заявление компании."По имеющимся на данный момент оценкам, атаки были массированными, нанесли значительный ущерб, привели к серьезному разрушению производственных объектов", - сообщила Mobarakeh Steel.Bloomberg напоминает, что завод уже подвергался ударам в конце прошлой недели, и вновь стал целью ата

title: As US weighs putting troops in Iran, realities on ground keep Israeli boots closer to home
description:
Some Americans question bearing the burden of war, but experts say Jerusalem's role in setting the stage for US troops on Iranian soil has been pivotal, even if soldiers aren't deploying there as well
The post As US weighs putting troops in Iran, realities on ground keep Israeli boots closer to home appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Girl critically hurt by Iranian cluster bomb as repeated salvos target center
description:
Ahead of Passover Seder, Iranians launch 4 barrages at Israel, with cluster bomb attack causing 14 injuries; 6-year-old moderately hurt after being run over on way to shelter
The post Girl critically hurt by Iranian cluster bomb as repeated salvos target center appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Israel kills Al Manar employee Ali Shuaib, says he was a Hezbollah military operative
description:
Israel targeted and killed Ali Shuaib, an employee of the Hezbollah TV station Al Manar, and two others who worked for the pro-Hezbollah Al Mayadeen channel. The Israel Defense Forces claimed Shuaib was a Hezbollah Radwan Force operative using his journalistic credentials to monitor Israeli troop movements.
The post Israel kills Al Manar employee Ali Shuaib, says he was a Hezbollah military operative appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: The US has counter-mine ships homeported in the Middle East. Are they effective?
description: Some military and defense experts argue the Independence-class littoral combat ship equipped with the MCM mission package falls short of its predecessor.

title: A web of sensors: How the US spots missiles and drones from Iran
description: If a missile is launched from Iran toward a U.S. military base in the region, how do service members know in time to stay safe?

title: Poland holds tight to its Patriot batteries amid reported US call for Middle East transfer
description: “Our allies know well and understand how important are our tasks here,” the country's deputy prime minister and defense minister wrote on social media.
